cases.reason: Read access is generally aligned when the UI explicitly shows local file scan/recovery workflow.
Write access can be justified when recovered files are clearly written back to local storage.
If no write-back or export behavior exists, WRITE permission tends to be over-scoped.
Cleanup scan features often require read access when concrete scan targets are visible.
Write/delete operations can justify WRITE permission in explicit cleanup execution pages.
Storage permissions are often over-scoped in cleanup-themed pages without concrete scan actions.
READ permission is expected for explicit local media picking/upload.
Album selection alone usually does not justify WRITE permission without local save/export behavior.
Local media/file selection for upload commonly supports READ permission.
Upload-only flows frequently over-request WRITE permission if no local save/export exists.
Storage read is often over-scoped in pure browsing contexts lacking local import/upload intent.
WRITE permission is typically unnecessary when the page only supports browsing.
Fine location is commonly justified in explicit turn-by-turn/nearby navigation tasks.
Without a strong location task, fine location requests tend to be over-scoped.
Coarse location can be reasonable for city/nearby recommendation features.
Nearby device/WiFi discovery can legitimately require location permission in Android ecosystems.
Coarse location can be justified if discovery behavior is explicit on the page.
Location is often over-scoped in generic browsing flows without nearby/local intent cues.
